notes.scope: Phase-3 Task 5 — commit b466277. Files in scope: app/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/engine/WarmupCoordinator.kt, app/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/di/AppModule.kt. Test file (WarmupCoordinatorTest.kt) not shipped and excluded from runtime audit.

threatsConsidered.threat: Injection via modelId string (OWASP Mobile M7 / CWE-20 / CWE-78)
vectors_checked: SQL/Room query parameter — modelId never reaches a Room query from this commit; DAO access paths are Task-4 code not modified here.

File path construction — WarmupCoordinator does no file I/O with modelId; registry.initialize uses allowlist-resolved Model objects, not the raw string, for file paths (DefaultModelRegistry resolves by model.name lookup on an in-memory allowlist list, not by concatenation).
Reflection / Class.forName — not used in the audited code or downstream initialize path.

Trust boundary — only two writers of default_model_id / last_used_model_id exist in the coordinator: (a) startDefaultModelObserver writes downloaded.model.modelId where downloaded comes from ModelRegistry.models which is populated exclusively from AllowlistLoader (bundled JSON asset); (b) launchWarmup writes setLastUsedModelId(modelId) only on registry.initialize success, which means the modelId matched a registry-loaded ModelEntry. A hostile DataStore value (e.g. manual root edit) would therefore fail registry.initialize lookup with IllegalArgumentException, surface through errorLog, and not propagate to any sensitive sink.

findings: ErrorLog.e("engine-warmup", "initialize failed for $modelId", cause) — modelId is a stable HF-style identifier like 'litert-community/gemma-4-E4B-it-litert-lm', not user-generated content and not PII.

ErrorLog sanitizes \n/\r/\t -> space and truncates description to 500 chars and cause.message to 200 chars (core-runtime/ErrorLog.kt:102-110), so a verbose native exception cannot exfiltrate secrets or corrupt the single-line format.
Log file is written to context.filesDir/logs/errors.log — app-private per Android scoped storage; not world-readable on non-rooted devices.
No tokens, auth credentials, or user message content are passed to this log site.

threatsConsidered.threat: Concurrency TOCTOU — double initialize holding lifecycleMutex (OWASP A04 / CWE-367)
analysis: launchWarmup (WarmupCoordinator.kt:112-131) wraps both warmupJob?.cancelAndJoin() and the subsequent scope.launch { registry.initialize(modelId) } inside restartMutex.withLock.

cancelAndJoin returns only after the old coroutine has fully exited — which means its registry.initialize call has returned and released DefaultModelRegistry.lifecycleMutex (core-runtime/DefaultModelRegistry.kt:201 withLock scope).
The new scope.launch body starts asynchronously, but it can only acquire lifecycleMutex after the old holder has released it; Mutex is strict FIFO and old + new never overlap.
_isWarmupInProgress is flipped to true before the new Job is assigned (line 117), closing the observable-state gap.
warmupDefault() and cancelAndRestart() both funnel through launchWarmup under the same restartMutex, so concurrent callers serialise correctly; there is no path that can bypass the mutex.
threatsConsidered.verdict: No TOCTOU window between old / new lifecycleMutex holders.

threatsConsidered.threat: Component exposure / cross-process reach (OWASP Mobile M8 / CWE-926)
analysis: AppModule is @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class) (AppModule.kt:29). Hilt's SingletonComponent is scoped to the Application instance of the process that owns it.

The :crash process (AndroidManifest.xml:49 android:process=":crash") runs in a separate Linux process with its own Application; Hilt bindings declared for SingletonComponent in the main process are not materialised there.
No exported Android component (activity/service/receiver/provider) references WarmupCoordinator or SanctumDatabase directly — they are purely in-process singletons.
WarmupCoordinator constructor is internal (with @VisibleForTesting) and has no IPC surface.

threatsConsidered.threat: Data-at-rest / insecure storage (OWASP Mobile M9 / CWE-312)
analysis: SanctumDatabase.create uses context.applicationContext and the name 'sanctum.db' (SanctumDatabase.kt:29-36), which resolves under /data/data/<pkg>/databases — app-private on non-rooted devices.

AppModule.provideSanctumDatabase injects @ApplicationContext and passes through SanctumDatabase.create (AppModule.kt:40-41) — no override of the default databases path, no allowBackup override in this file, no plaintext export path.
PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON in onOpen (SanctumDatabase.kt:38-43) is a data-integrity control, not a security regression.
No additional storage controls expected at this layer per scope notes — full-disk encryption is provided by Android's FBE/FDE for the databases directory.
threatsConsidered.verdict: Storage posture unchanged from Task 1 / Phase-1 baseline.

threatsConsidered.threat: Insecure design — one-shot observer racing self-cancel (OWASP A04)
analysis: startDefaultModelObserver uses ownJob.cancel() after appSettings.setDefaultModelId(...) inside the collect lambda (WarmupCoordinator.kt:141-149).

The getDefaultModelId().isEmpty() guard means a benign second emission with the setting already populated becomes a no-op; even if cancel is racy, at-most-once-effective semantics are preserved by the guard — not a security defect.
threatsConsidered.verdict: No security impact (correctness already covered by code-reviewer scope).

analysis: No credentials, tokens, keys, or connection strings in either file. 'sanctum.db' is a filename, 'engine-warmup' is a log-component tag enforced by whitelist in ErrorLog.ALLOWED_COMPONENTS.

No JSON.parse / ObjectInputStream / SnakeYAML usage. DataStore-proto (protobuf) deserialization is in core-settings and was audited in Task 2 scope; modelId strings read back are opaque String values, not polymorphic objects.
WarmupCoordinator performs no network I/O. registry.initialize is purely a native-engine warmup; downloads happen elsewhere and are gated by the allowlist.

owaspTop10_2021_mapping.A01_BrokenAccessControl: N/A — no authz decisions in scope.
owaspTop10_2021_mapping.A04_InsecureDesign: Clean — mutex + cancelAndJoin ordering is correct.
owaspTop10_2021_mapping.A05_SecurityMisconfiguration: Clean — Hilt scoping correct, Room builder minimal but safe.
owaspTop10_2021_mapping.A06_VulnerableComponents: No new Gradle deps per task spec.
owaspTop10_2021_mapping.A08_SoftwareDataIntegrity: Clean — no deserialization of untrusted data.
owaspTop10_2021_mapping.A09_LoggingMonitoring: Clean — modelId logged is non-PII; ErrorLog sanitizes and truncates.
